# Anti-Patterns — Marketing Traps

**Anti-Pattern 1: Tech for Tech's Sake. Adopting technology without a clear purpose.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Data Without Action. Collecting data but not using it.**

**Case: The Robot. Using AI to replace human interaction entirely. Customers hate it.**

**Case: The Creepy Factor. Personalization that feels invasive. Bad data practices.**

**Case: The Rigid Plan. An annual marketing plan that cannot adapt to change.**

**Case: The Silos. Data trapped in separate departments. No unified view.**
---

**Case: The Spam. Personalized emails can feel like spam if done wrong.**
**Case: The Over-automation. Automating everything removes the human touch.**
**Case: The Short-term. Focusing only on short-term metrics hurts long-term brand.**
**Case: The Vanity. Chasing vanity metrics like likes and views.**
**Case: The Intrusion. Interrupting customers with irrelevant messages is counterproductive.**
**Case: The Assumption. Assuming all customers want the same thing is a mistake.**
**Case: The Cookie. Relying on third-party cookies is ending. First-party data is the future.**
**Case: The Channel. Focusing on one channel while ignoring others misses opportunities.**
**Case: The Scale. Personalization must scale. Manual personalization does not work.**
